content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Role-Swapping Prompts for Real-Time Social Bias Mitigation in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) often generate outputs reflecting entrenched social biases and stereotypes, particularly when producing text about individuals from marginalized groups. Existing bias mitigation techniques in prompting are limited in their ability to address intersectional biases and rarely adaptively debias in real-time generation.",
  "motivation": "Mitigating social biases in LLM output is critical for fair and ethical AI deployment, especially as LLMs are increasingly used in high-stakes, user-facing applications. Prior approaches typically require model retraining, chain-of-thought interventions, or external knowledge injection, but there is a need for a prompt-based method that is model-agnostic, requires no parameter updates, and can address nuanced, intersectional biases on-the-fly.",
  "method": "We propose a novel prompting framework that, for any input involving individuals or social groups, automatically generates a set of counterfactual role-swapped prompts—systematically interchanging demographic attributes (e.g., gender, ethnicity, religion) while preserving all other context. The LLM is prompted to generate outputs for both the original and all counterfactual versions, then guided to explicitly compare and justify differences among the outputs. Finally, the LLM is instructed to synthesize a debiased response by minimizing unjustified disparities revealed through this process. The entire sequence is implemented through prompt engineering alone, requiring no access to model weights or external datasets.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on bias-sensitive datasets such as StereoSet and the CrowS-Pairs benchmark, comparing our method against standard prompting, self-debiasing, and chain-of-thought debiasing. Metrics include stereotyping scores, consistency across counterfactuals, and human-rated fairness. Ablation studies will analyze the contribution of counterfactual diversity and explicit comparison steps."
}
